name: One-Way Platform
description: A platform blocks movement from selected approach directions while allowing entry or exit through other directions.
design_notes: One-way platforms are collision filters with strong player expectations. They need per-actor ignore windows, approach-side tests, surface normal rules, and drop-through handling so upward movement, falling landings, enemies, projectiles, and moving platforms do not contradict each other.
design_purpose: Increase vertical route density without fully enclosing the player.
Let players jump through floors and land on top surfaces in layered arenas.
Support drop-through movement, enemy routing, and platforming shortcuts with readable collision behavior.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can pass through platforms naturally while still trusting landings from above.

parameters.name: landing_normal_min_y
parameters.description: Minimum upward surface normal required before the contact can become a landing.

parameters.name: pass_through_margin_units
parameters.description: Clearance tolerance used while entering from below so thin platforms do not snag the actor.

parameters.name: drop_ignore_window_ms
parameters.description: Per-actor collision ignore window after intentional drop-through input is accepted.

parameters.name: actor_ignore_token_id
parameters.description: Token that prevents global collider toggles and scopes pass-through behavior to one actor.

parameters.name: previous_position_sample_id
parameters.description: Sample used to decide whether the actor approached from above or crossed from below.

edge_cases: Fast upward movement must not snag on the platform underside or become a false landing after tunneling.
A moving one-way platform changes contact normals while carrying the player sideways or downward.
Characters spawn or load inside the platform and need a recovery rule before collision becomes solid.
Drop-through input is buffered just before landing and should not immediately cancel the landing unintentionally.
common_bugs: Collision toggles globally and makes the platform non-solid for every actor instead of only the dropping actor.
Previous-frame position is sampled after movement, so the approach-side test always thinks the actor came from above.
Drop-through re-enables before the actor clears platform thickness and catches them in a loop.
Enemies, projectiles, and players all share one collision rule even though design expects different behavior.
balancing_notes: Dense one-way layouts support vertical combat but can become visually confusing without consistent platform art.
Drop-through delay should be long enough to avoid re-catching but short enough to allow quick recovery.
If enemies use different collision rules, teach that behavior before it matters in combat.
accessibility_notes: Use consistent visual language for semisolid platforms so players can identify collision behavior before jumping.
Allow remappable or hold-based drop-through input for players who have difficulty pressing down plus jump quickly.

implementation_notes.unity: PlatformEffector2D handles many 2D cases, but custom controllers should filter contacts per actor using previous feet position, contact_normal, and drop_ignore_window_ms. Avoid disabling the shared collider globally; use Physics2D.IgnoreCollision or contact modification scoped to actor_ignore_token_id.
implementation_notes.godot: One-way collision on CollisionShape2D is useful for standard cases. For custom controllers, accept the platform only when the previous position sample was above the top plane and no per-actor drop token is active.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a platform component exposing top plane, approach tests, and per-character ignore tokens. CharacterMovement sweeps can reject contacts from below, while replicated drop-through should send the accepted input tick so server and client ignore windows match.
implementation_notes.web: Resolve the platform as a segment or slab that blocks only downward crossings from above. Store drop_ignore_window_ms per actor, sample previous feet position before integration, and keep moving platform deltas in the same collision pass.

example_games.title: Super Mario World
example_games.note: Uses semisolid platforms that can be jumped through and stood on.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses pass-through platforms in precision platforming layouts.
